site.title: Minecraft Mod MCP
site.tagline: Let an AI agent actually play your Minecraft — to test your mods
site.description: An in-game mod + CLI that exposes a running Minecraft to AI agents over MCP. The agent sees the screen, clicks, types, launches a test client, and frees the mouse so you can keep working in your tool.
site.slogan: Test mods at the speed of thought

footer.copyright: © {year} Minecraft Mod MCP. Open source under MIT / Apache-2.0 / CC0-1.0.

cap.heading: Four things an agent can do
cap.intro: Each maps to a real, verifiable capability — not a marketing slogan.

actuate.title: Drive the live game
actuate.desc: Through Java reflection the agent clicks, types, scrolls, drags and presses keys on a running client — working across every supported version with no per-version code.
points: Inspect any open screen and its buttons
Run commands and query debug fields

bootstrap.title: Resolve deps & launch
bootstrap.desc: The CLI auto-detects Java, downloads the requested Minecraft version and mod loader, then boots a test client — one command from a clean machine to a controlled game.
points: Fetches the version + loader on demand
Launches client, server, or both together

detach.title: Release the mouse, keep control
detach.desc: An in-game overlay (top-right) detaches the cursor so the game won't auto-pause when you tab away — the MCP connection stays live while you drive your AI tool.
points: Top-right overlay button in the 3D view
Alt+Tab to your IDE or agent without pausing
MCP commands keep flowing the whole time

see.title: Screenshot with a grid
see.desc: Captures the viewport overlaid with a coordinate grid so the agent knows exactly where things are — the foundation for vision-driven autonomy.
points: Pairs with a vision model for full autonomy

loop.heading: Agent ⇄ Bridge ⇄ Game
loop.intro: The detach overlay is what makes a real dev loop possible — your AI tool and the game coexist instead of fighting for focus.

tip.detach: Click the top-right overlay to free the cursor
tip.alive: The bridge keeps the connection alive while you're away
tip.alttab: Alt+Tab to your tool, then back — no re-triggered pause

vision.heading: Best with a vision model
vision.title: A grid the model can reason over
vision.desc: See is useful on its own — but pair it with a multimodal model (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini, Qwen-VL…) and the agent can read the screen, locate UI, and decide its next action without you in the loop.
vision.caption: // capture + grid → the model sees coordinates, not just pixels

point.grid: The grid gives every pixel an address the model can cite
point.model: A vision model turns the frame into a plan: where to click next
point.autonomy: See + Actuate + a model = a self-driving test loop

quickstart.heading: From zero to agent-driven
quickstart.oneLink: Already installed? One link is all your AI agent needs — paste it and it self-configures.

step1.title: Install the bridge
step1.desc: The npm package is the CLI + MCP bridge in one.

step2.title: Launch a test client
step2.desc: Java, the version and the loader are resolved automatically.

step3.title: Detach the mouse
step3.desc: Click the top-right in-game overlay so the connection survives Alt+Tab.

step4.title: Connect your AI
step4.desc: Point Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Copilot or any MCP tool at the bridge.

loader.heading: Compatibility
loader.intro: Reflection means a single codebase runs across every supported Minecraft version.
